title: EcoSortNet — Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion Waste Sorting System
description: My MSc capstone project (supervised by Mr. Goh Hock Guan). A hierarchical waste classification prototype that fuses spectral data, laser ranging and inductive proximity sensing to autonomously sort waste into four categories on a conveyor belt.

I designed the full hardware circuit topology independently — MG996R and SG90 servo control, camera pipeline, AI classification integration, conveyor mechanics, anti-jam deflector and funnel system — and wrote all Python control logic from scratch.

title: Raspberry Pi Sensor Debug Toolkit
description: A personal collection of tested Python drivers, I2C diagnostic scripts and wiring reference sheets for the sensors I've worked with: AS7343 (spectral), VL53L0X (ToF ranging), LJC18A3 (inductive proximity) and common servo controllers. Saves hours every time I start a new hardware project.

title: EcoSortNet Dashboard — Real-time Sensor Data Visualisation
description: The frontend control panel for EcoSortNet. Built with Vue 3 Composition API and Tailwind CSS, it displays live sensor readings (spectral channels, distance, proximity state), classification results, sorting statistics and system health — all updating in real time via WebSocket from the Raspberry Pi backend.

title: Campus Wi-Fi Captive Portal Redesign
description: Redesigned a university campus Wi-Fi authentication portal. Replaced the legacy form with a clean Vue.js SPA backed by a lightweight Node.js proxy. Focused on mobile responsiveness, fast load on weak connections and clear bilingual (EN/ZH) copy.

title: VHACK_TrustGuard-AI — AI Safety & Trust Protection
description: Built with my team at USM Varsity Hackathon 2026. An open-source framework for AI trust and safety verification. I was responsible for the core logic and system integration: the threat classification pipeline, API layer and integration with the frontend trust-score dashboard. Competed against 40+ university teams.

title: Personal AI Agent Infrastructure on Vercel
description: My own production AI agent setup — a Vercel-deployed backend handling LLM API calls, prompt management, rate limiting and response caching, wired to a Vue.js frontend dashboard. This is the infrastructure backbone I use across multiple projects and experiments.
